title: Paternity Leave: Progress and Persistent Gaps
subtitle: 陪产假为何推不动：职场爸爸的两难困境

paragraphs.en: Attitudes towards paternity leave have drastically changed in America in the last five years as more fathers feel comfortable taking extended time off, but gender bias persists when it comes to career prospects and the home, according to a new study of working parents.
paragraphs.zh: 一项针对在职父母的最新研究显示，过去五年里，随着越来越多的父亲愿意休更长的陪产假，美国对陪产假的态度已发生巨大变化，但在职业前景和家庭责任方面，性别偏见依然存在。

paragraphs.en: Research by the Boston College Center for Work & Family, which surveyed new parents at four large U.S. companies who were qualified for taking at least six weeks paid parental leave, found that 81% of the 1,240 employees surveyed said the notion of fathers taking leave has become more acceptable.
paragraphs.zh: 波士顿学院工作与家庭中心调查了四家美国大公司中符合至少六周带薪育儿假资格的新手父母，发现在1240名受访员工中，81%的人表示父亲休育儿假这一观念已变得更容易被接受。

paragraphs.en: Of those surveyed, 62% of fathers took the maximum amount of time off compared to 93% of mothers, and around three-quarters of workers said their employer was equally supportive of mothers and fathers taking parental leave and over half said leave policies had made workplace culture better.
paragraphs.zh: 在受访者中，62%的父亲休满了最长假期，而母亲的比例为93%；约四分之三的员工表示雇主对母亲和父亲休育儿假同样支持，超过一半的人称休假政策改善了职场文化。

words.meaning: adj. 最大限度的，最多的

paragraphs.en: The U.S. is one of only three countries in the world not to offer statutory paid leave, but increasingly states and companies are starting to take up the issue. So far, eight states and the District of Columbia have their own paid family leave laws.
paragraphs.zh: 美国是世界上仅有的三个不提供法定带薪育儿假的国家之一，但越来越多的州和公司开始着手解决这一问题。目前已有八个州和哥伦比亚特区颁布了自己的带薪家庭假法律。

words.meaning: adj. 法定的，依照法令的

paragraphs.en: Brad Harrington, executive director of the center and lead author of the study, estimates only 20% to 30% of companies in the U.S. offer paid parental leave. He feels the research findings reflect an obvious change in corporate attitudes to new fathers taking time off.
paragraphs.zh: 该中心执行主任兼本研究第一作者布拉德·哈林顿估计，美国仅有20%至30%的公司提供带薪育儿假。他认为研究结果反映出企业对新晋父亲休假的态度发生了明显转变。

paragraphs.en: We did a study on paternity leave five years ago. Compared with those findings, these numbers were shocking to me. I did not expect 80% of people to say the organization finds dads taking this leave acceptable and three-quarters to say it's equally supportive of women and men taking leave, Harrington said.
paragraphs.zh: 哈林顿说：“五年前我们做过一项陪产假研究。与那时的结果相比，这次的数据令我震惊。我没想到80%的人会表示公司认为父亲休陪产假是可以接受的，更没想到四分之三的人会说公司对男女休假同样支持。”

words.meaning: n. 调查结果，研究发现

paragraphs.en: The previous study found that nearly three-quarters of fathers saw two to four weeks as an appropriate duration for paternity leave and 76% said they would prefer not to take all their time off at once.
paragraphs.zh: 此前的研究发现，近四分之三的父亲认为两到四周是陪产假的合适时长，76%的人表示不愿一次性休完所有假期。

paragraphs.en: Since then, there have been a number of legal cases against companies involving paternity leave — including cases against JPMorgan Chase and Estée Lauder — which have helped put pressure on companies to make their parental leave policies gender neutral.
paragraphs.zh: 自那以后，出现了多起涉及陪产假的公司诉讼案——包括针对摩根大通和雅诗兰黛的案件——这些诉讼促使企业面临压力，将其育儿假政策调整为性别中立。

paragraphs.en: However, the study also shows how traditional gender roles endure both at work, where more women than men reported changes in their perceived career potential, and at home — even among workers who claim to have a strong desire for equality.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，研究也表明传统性别角色在职场和家庭中依然根深蒂固：职场中有更多女性报告称自己感知到的职业潜力发生了变化，而家庭中——即便是那些声称强烈渴望平等的员工之间——也是如此。

paragraphs.en: The vast majority of men, 97%, said one of the top reasons to take leave was to share caregiving with their partner. But when they were asked about how caregiving and household tasks were divided, their answers painted a different picture. While about 75% of employees said both genders should give the same amount of care, the majority of men and nearly half of women admitted that in reality the female actually did most of it. A tiny fraction, 2%, of men said they did more of the childcare.
paragraphs.zh: 绝大多数男性（97%）表示，休假的首要原因之一是与伴侣分担照护责任。但当被问及照护和家务如何分工时，他们的回答却呈现出另一番景象。尽管约75%的员工认为男女应承担同等的照护责任，但大多数男性和近半数女性承认，现实中大部分照护工作仍由女性完成。仅有极少数男性（2%）表示自己承担了更多的育儿工作。

paragraphs.en: Men and women's experiences of the return to work following parental leave were also considerably different. Of the women surveyed, 32% reported a downturn in their job satisfaction, while 14% said it increased. In comparison, 17% of men said their job satisfaction went down and 20% said it went up. Meanwhile, more women reported an increase in their responsibilities and manager expectations after childbirth. Half of the women said they used flexible work arrangements after becoming a parent, while just 27% of men did. Similar percentages of men and women said they enjoyed their careers and that it gave them a sense of achievement, while around half of women and 44% of men said it was a key part of their identity.
paragraphs.zh: 男性和女性在育儿假后重返工作的体验也大相径庭。在受访女性中，32%表示工作满意度下降，14%表示有所上升。相比之下，17%的男性称工作满意度下降，20%称上升。与此同时，更多女性报告称生育后责任加重、上司期望提高。半数女性表示成为父母后采用了弹性工作安排，而男性中仅有27%这样做。男女中均有相似比例的人表示热爱自己的职业并从中获得成就感，约半数女性和44%的男性称职业是自我认同的关键部分。

paragraphs.en: On the subject of career advancement, 59% of women and 49% of men said leave could be limiting and both genders said they feared it would have an impact on their progress long-term. But on opportunity for promotion, more than double the number of women, 30% compared with 15% of men, believed their chances to be lower after becoming a parent. Despite progress, the struggle for women to reach the highest positions of power is demonstrated in this year's Fortune 500 list, which featured a record 33 female CEOs, but this still represents a tiny fraction of the total.
paragraphs.zh: 在职业发展方面，59%的女性和49%的男性认为休假可能会带来限制，且男女双方都担心这会对长期发展产生影响。但在晋升机会上，认为成为父母后晋升几率降低的女性比例是男性的两倍多（30%对15%）。尽管有所进步，今年《财富》500强榜单仍彰显了女性攀登最高权力职位的艰难——榜单上有创纪录的33位女性CEO，但这仍只占总数的极小部分。

paragraphs.en: Harrington said culture change depends on companies putting more focus on men and their responsibilities. By that I mean companies need to give men paternity leave and encourage men to take time off to be with their kids early on in the kids' life. They also need to recognize that men have to make significant adjustments when they become parents. Companies cannot do all these things to enhance women's advancement and then turn around and say, 'Oh, but we don't expect the men to take over for the women at home.'
paragraphs.zh: 哈林顿表示，文化变革取决于企业给予男性及其家庭责任更多关注。“我的意思是，公司需要为男性提供陪产假，并鼓励他们在孩子生命早期花时间陪伴。他们还需认识到，男性成为父亲后也必须做出重大调整。公司不能一边做各种事促进女性发展，一边又说‘哦，但我们不指望男性在家接替女性的工作’。”

paragraphs.en: In May, the American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) and Outten & Golden LLP announced a historic class-action $5m settlement with JPMorgan Chase on behalf of male employees who claim they were illegally denied access to paid parental leave. Derek Rotondo, 35, filed the discrimination charge against his company after he was allegedly told by his HR department that mothers were considered primary caregivers. Thus, they were allowed to take 16 weeks of paid parental leave. Fathers, however, could take just two weeks. The father of two from Columbus, Ohio, who still works at the company as an associate and investigator, said he has witnessed a domino effect across companies since the settlement, but that there is still substantial progress to be made towards changing attitudes towards paternity leave. I do think there's still some way to go. There's still going to be sort of the unstated expectation for new dads to essentially come right back to work, but I think the research is showing that's starting to change. He said equal parental leave is an essential component to creating gender equality in the workplace. The old standard of women staying home, having babies and cooking doesn't apply and hasn't applied for a long time.
paragraphs.zh: 今年五月，美国公民自由联盟（ACLU）与Outten & Golden律师事务所代表声称被非法剥夺带薪育儿假的男性员工，宣布与摩根大通达成了五百万美元的历史性集体诉讼和解。35岁的德里克·罗通多在据称被人力资源部门告知母亲被视为主要照护者后，对公司提起了歧视指控——母亲可休16周带薪育儿假，而父亲只能休两周。这位来自俄亥俄州哥伦布市的两个孩子的父亲目前仍在该公司担任助理调查员，他表示自和解以来，各公司间出现了“多米诺效应”，但在改变对陪产假的态度方面仍有很大进步空间。“我确实认为还有一段路要走……对新爸爸来说，那种‘最好立刻回去上班’的不成文期望依然存在，但我认为研究显示情况正在开始改变。”他说平等的育儿假是在职场实现性别平等的关键组成部分。“女性待在家里生孩子做饭的旧标准早就不适用了，而且很久以前就不适用了。”
